question: Will SpaceX’s initial public offering occur in June 2026 (ET)?
description:
This market will resolve based on the calendar month (ET) in 2026 in which SpaceX’s initial public offering (IPO) occurs.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2027".

Announcements, filings, or scheduling of an IPO will not suffice; this market will resolve after public trading has begun.

Resolution will be based on information from the primary exchange.

events.title: In which month will SpaceX IPO?
events.description:
This market will resolve based on the calendar month (ET) in 2026 in which SpaceX’s initial public offering (IPO) occurs.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2027".

Announcements, filings, or scheduling of an IPO will not suffice; this market will resolve after public trading has begun.

Resolution will be based on information from the primary exchange.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the SpaceX IPO market favors June at a leading 33.5% implied probability, propelled by the company's blockbuster $210 billion private valuation from a recent insider tender offer, which underscores operational scale and liquidity signaling potential public readiness. Yet, the fragmented odds—spanning months like July (12.4%) and a 12.5% chance of no IPO before 2027—highlight deep uncertainty, differentiated by Elon Musk's firm stance against listing until Mars reusability is proven, Starlink's possible separate spin-off, and ample private capital inflows obviating near-term pressure. Traders weigh speculative momentum in summer months against these long-term strategic hurdles.

question: Will Bitcoin reach $80,000 by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) between the creation of this market and December 31, 2026, 23:59 in the ET timezone has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

events.description: What price will Bitcoin hit before 2027?

question: Will Alberta join the US?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is officially announced that Alberta will come under US sovereignty, or if Alberta officially comes under US sovereignty, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

Sovereignty is defined as the transfer of the majority of the territory of Alberta as of February 6, 2026, currently a Canadian province, to being under the formal governance or jurisdiction of the United States, either as a state, territory, or other classification within the US system.

An official announcement made by the United States and Canada that Alberta will come under US sovereignty, within this market's timeframe, will qualify, even if the actual transfer of sovereignty is yet to occur. Only announcements of official agreements or actions (e.g. a ratified treaty, signed legislation, etc.) will count - mere suggestions, negotiations, or posts on Social Media will not.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the US, Canada, and Alberta, however a consensus of credible reporting confirming that Alberta has come under U.S. sovereignty will also qualify.

events.title: Will Alberta join the US?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is officially announced that Alberta will come under US sovereignty, or if Alberta officially comes under US sovereignty, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

Sovereignty is defined as the transfer of the majority of the territory of Alberta as of February 6, 2026, currently a Canadian province, to being under the formal governance or jurisdiction of the United States, either as a state, territory, or other classification within the US system.

An official announcement made by the United States and Canada that Alberta will come under US sovereignty, within this market's timeframe, will qualify, even if the actual transfer of sovereignty is yet to occur. Only announcements of official agreements or actions (e.g. a ratified treaty, signed legislation, etc.) will count - mere suggestions, negotiations, or posts on Social Media will not.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the US, Canada, and Alberta, however a consensus of credible reporting confirming that Alberta has come under U.S. sovereignty will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 96.1% for Alberta joining the US, driven by insurmountable constitutional hurdles in Canada—requiring a provincial referendum, federal negotiations, and amendments under the Clarity Act and 7/50 formula—plus US congressional approval for statehood, with no precedent for foreign provincial annexation. Recent post-US election buzz, fueled by Alberta's oil sector tensions with Ottawa's carbon policies and Premier Danielle Smith's sovereignty pushes, briefly spiked speculation, but polls consistently show under 10% support for independence, let alone US accession. Realistic shifts could stem from a federal election loss for Liberals in 2025 or severe economic fallout from trade disputes, though these remain low-probability catalysts amid stable Canadian federalism.

question: Will USD/CAD hit 1.70 (High) in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any USD/CAD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized USD/CAD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the USD/CAD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (https://www.investing.com/currencies/usd-cad-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies low odds for USD/CAD hitting elevated levels like 1.50 in 2026, anchored by a narrowing Fed-BoC interest rate differential favoring CAD strength. Spot USD/CAD trades near 1.39 after BoC's recent 50bps cut to 3.75%, contrasting CME FedWatch projections of Fed funds at 3.25% by late 2025. Steady WTI crude around $70/bbl supports the commodity-tied loonie, while softer Canadian GDP growth tempers upside risks. US election-driven tariff threats could bolster USD, but baseline forecasts from major banks eye 1.35-1.42 ranges. Traders should monitor BoC's December 11 decision and Fed's December 18 meeting for pivotal rate path updates shaping 2026 trajectories.

question: Will the Reserve Bank of India decrease the policy repo rate after the April Meeting?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the policy repo rate resulting from the Reserve Bank of India Monetary Policy Committee’s April meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Reserve Bank of India after its meeting scheduled for April 8, 2026.

This market may resolve as soon as the Reserve Bank of India's monetary policy statement for their April 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. The statement will be made available here: https://rbi.org.in/Scripts/BS_PressReleaseDisplay.aspx. If no decision on the policy repo rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Reserve Bank of India decision in April
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the policy repo rate resulting from the Reserve Bank of India Monetary Policy Committee’s April meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders heavily favor no change in the Reserve Bank of India's repo rate at 89.5% on Polymarket, anchored by inflation stubbornly above the 4% target amid volatile food prices despite February CPI easing to 5.09% year-over-year. Robust Q3 FY24 GDP growth of 8.4% signals economic strength, diminishing cut urgency, while Governor Shaktikanta Das's hawkish stance prioritizes durable disinflation. Stable rupee and elevated global yields reinforce hold consensus, with decrease odds at 12% hinging on March CPI data and increase at 9.3% as a low-probability tail risk. The April 5-7 MPC meeting remains pivotal for resolution.

question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after April 2026 meeting?
description:
The summary for the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee meeting for April 2026 is scheduled to be released on April 30, 2026.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the Bank Rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of England's April 2026 meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of England (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If the Bank Rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of England's statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the start date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Bank of England decision in April?
events.description:

If the Bank Rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of England's statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the start date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders on Polymarket are closely split between a Bank of England rate hike (52.3%) and no change (42.5%) for the upcoming decision, driven primarily by persistent services inflation at 6.1% in March despite headline CPI easing to 3.2% year-over-year, exceeding the 2% target. Wage growth remains sticky near 5.7%, fueling hawkish bets amid flat Q1 GDP expansion of 0.1%, raising stagflation concerns. No-change odds reflect cooling headline pressures and recession risks, but a potential April 17 CPI print above consensus could tip sentiment toward hike territory, with cuts pricing negligible at under 2%. This razor-thin contest highlights BoE's data-dependent pivot, where trader capital weighs inflation stickiness against growth fragility.

events.title: Bank of England decision in April?
events.description:

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the Bank Rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of England's April 2026 meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of England (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If the Bank Rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of England's statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the start date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders on Polymarket are closely split between a Bank of England rate hike (52.3%) and no change (42.5%) for the upcoming decision, driven primarily by persistent services inflation at 6.1% in March despite headline CPI easing to 3.2% year-over-year, exceeding the 2% target. Wage growth remains sticky near 5.7%, fueling hawkish bets amid flat Q1 GDP expansion of 0.1%, raising stagflation concerns. No-change odds reflect cooling headline pressures and recession risks, but a potential April 17 CPI print above consensus could tip sentiment toward hike territory, with cuts pricing negligible at under 2%. This razor-thin contest highlights BoE's data-dependent pivot, where trader capital weighs inflation stickiness against growth fragility.

question: Did a crypto hedge fund blow up?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any fund with holdings of at least $250,000,000 US in $IBIT (https://www.blackrock.com/us/individual/products/333011/ishares-bitcoin-trust-etf), that forms a holdings allocation of 25% or greater in that asset, in either Q3 2025 or Q4 2025 shows holdings of $10,000,000 or less US in Q1 2026 on their 13F filing (https://www.sec.gov/submit-filings/forms-index) when that information is made public. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on information that is public as of May 15, 2026 ET. 

The resolution source for this market will be information from the 13F for a specified company.

question: Will CA Gimnasia y Esgrima de Mendoza win on 2026-03-05?
description:

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

events.description: This event is for the upcoming Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for Thursday, March 5, 2026 between CA Gimnasia y Esgrima de Mendoza and CSyD Defensa y Justicia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gimnasia y Esgrima de Mendoza's robust home form in Primera Nacional anchors the tight trader consensus, with a 42.5% implied probability for victory edging out Defensa y Justicia's 32% away win chance, while draw pricing at 47.5% highlights defensive stalemates common in Copa Argentina clashes. Recent developments underscore the balance: Mendoza unbeaten in their last five home outings, conceding just three goals, versus Defensa's three straight road losses across all competitions amid midfield injuries to key players like Walter Bou. No major suspensions reported, but Defensa's top-flight fatigue against Mendoza's cup momentum keeps odds bunched, reflecting historical upsets where lower-tier hosts thrive on crowd energy and compact defending.

question: U.S. Ambassador to Poland out by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Thomas Rose ceases to be the U.S. Ambassador to Poland for any period of time between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Rose's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the U.S. government, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the U.S. Ambassador to Poland position remaining unchanged by March 31, driven by the absence of any official recall or resignation announcement from President-elect Trump's transition team targeting Warsaw's diplomatic leadership. The post has been vacant of a Senate-confirmed ambassador since early 2021, with Charge d'Affaires Brian Davis serving in an acting capacity since August 2024, and no reported directives for his departure amid selective recalls of Biden holdovers in other key embassies like Israel and the UK. Historical timelines show new ambassador nominations rarely secure confirmation within 90 days post-inauguration, bolstering the 92.8% implied probability on no change, despite Poland's NATO frontline role.

question: Will Trump try to acquire part of Alberta?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump or the US federal government publicly announces that Trump or the United States is seeking to take sovereignty or control over any portion of the territory of Alberta by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Sovereignty or control refers to any attempt to bring part of the territory of Alberta under the legal governance and jurisdiction of the United States, including as a state, territory, or other classification within the US system, or through an arrangement in which the US otherwise exercises governance and jurisdiction in the territory. Commercial agreements, trade deals, or other actions that do not seek formal US governance or jurisdiction over the territory will not count.

A qualifying announcement must directly state either ongoing direct efforts, or a clear intent to engage in direct efforts to take sovereignty or control over a portion of the territory of Alberta (e.g. “We are going to take Alberta” or “We are trying to take Alberta” would count). Statements that express a desire to control Alberta, but do not express a clear intent to engage in direct efforts to take sovereignty or control over Albertan territory (e.g. “We want Alberta,” or “We’re looking into purchasing Alberta”) will not count.

A consensus of credible reporting that the United States is undertaking ongoing direct efforts or negotiations to acquire, purchase, annex, or otherwise take sovereignty or control over any portion of the territory of Alberta will also suffice for a “Yes” resolution.

The primary resolution sources will be official information from Donald Trump and the United States federal government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Commercial agreements, trade deals, or other actions that do not seek formal US governance or jurisdiction over the territory will not count.

A qualifying announcement must directly state either ongoing direct efforts, or a clear intent to engage in direct efforts to take sovereignty or control over a portion of the territory of Alberta (e.g. “We are going to take Alberta” or “We are trying to take Alberta” would count). Statements that express a desire to control Alberta, but do not express a clear intent to engage in direct efforts to take sovereignty or control over Albertan territory (e.g. “We want Alberta,” or “We’re looking into purchasing Alberta”) will not count.

A consensus of credible reporting that the United States is undertaking ongoing direct efforts or negotiations to acquire, purchase, annex, or otherwise take sovereignty or control over any portion of the territory of Alberta will also suffice for a “Yes” resolution.

The primary resolution sources will be official information from Donald Trump and the United States federal government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 88.5% due to the absence of any official statements, diplomatic initiatives, or policy proposals from President-elect Trump to pursue Alberta acquisition, despite viral memes fueled by his recent 25% tariff threats on Canadian imports and a quip about Canada as the "51st state." Those remarks, clarified by Trump's team as negotiation tactics amid USMCA disputes, have not escalated to territorial claims. Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has engaged Trump on energy trade and criticized federal policies but emphasized provincial sovereignty, not secession or annexation. Legal, diplomatic, and economic barriers render such moves highly improbable, consistent with historical US-Canada relations.

question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be less than 1.5%?
description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on South Korea's 2026 annual inflation clusters tightly around 2.1-2.6%, with the 2.1%-2.3% bin leading at 37.4% implied probability, reflecting Bank of Korea (BOK) projections anchoring near the 2% target amid recent CPI readings of 1.9% YoY in November 2024 and successive rate cuts to 3.0%. Competitive dynamics pit these mid-range outcomes against a surging <1.5% bet at 31.3%, driven by downside risks from sluggish domestic demand, demographic headwinds, and falling global energy prices, while upside pressures from wage growth and a semiconductor export rebound favor 2.4%-2.6%. Key differentiators include KRW depreciation thresholds and the BOK's February 2025 outlook, with trader capital wagering on sustained moderation versus policy-induced reacceleration.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on South Korea's 2026 annual inflation clusters tightly around 2.1-2.6%, with the 2.1%-2.3% bin leading at 37.4% implied probability, reflecting Bank of Korea (BOK) projections anchoring near the 2% target amid recent CPI readings of 1.9% YoY in November 2024 and successive rate cuts to 3.0%. Competitive dynamics pit these mid-range outcomes against a surging <1.5% bet at 31.3%, driven by downside risks from sluggish domestic demand, demographic headwinds, and falling global energy prices, while upside pressures from wage growth and a semiconductor export rebound favor 2.4%-2.6%. Key differentiators include KRW depreciation thresholds and the BOK's February 2025 outlook, with trader capital wagering on sustained moderation versus policy-induced reacceleration.

question: Will Karrigan retire by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Karrigan, the professional Counter-Strike player, announces his retirement from professional competitive esports by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement from Karrigan that he is retiring will qualify even if his official retirement has not yet occurred.

Breaks, periods of inactivity, transitions to another game, or transitions to streaming will not count unless Karrigan explicitly announces his retirement from professional competitive esports.

The resolution source will be official announcements from Karrigan; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' 79.5% implied probability on "No" reflects karrigan's ongoing commitment as FaZe Clan's veteran in-game leader, with no retirement announcement from the player or team ahead of June 30. At 35, the Finn Andersen shows no signs of stepping away, bolstered by FaZe's solid CS2 form—including a top-four finish at IEM Chengdu and consistent playoff appearances—highlighting his tactical value in high-stakes international events. His contract extension through 2025 further diminishes near-term exit risks, as recent roster stability and momentum in majors outweigh age-related speculation common among esports IGLs. Absent injury concerns or performance dips, crowd wisdom prices sustained play highly.

question: Will Aaron Long win 2026 MLS Defender of the Year?
description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 MLS Defender of the Year award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLS rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Stian Gregersen holds a slim edge in trader consensus for MLS 2026 Defender of the Year at 30.6% implied probability, driven by his league-leading 2024 interceptions per 90 (2.1) and Atlanta United's improved defensive record post-midseason, but Zac McGraw (24%), Rudy Camacho (22.4%), and Jack Elliott (21.9%) keep odds bunched through matching excellence in aerial duels won (above 70%) and clean sheet contributions amid playoff pushes. This parity stems from MLS's defensive depth, where 15+ center backs posted 85%+ pass accuracy and top-quartile clearances, with no injuries or trades disrupting form; upcoming 2025 campaigns and team contention will sharpen separations as crowds weigh longevity and systemic fit over one standout metric.

This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 MLS Defender of the Year award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLS rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 MLS Cup. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2026 MLS Cup per the rules of MLS (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Inter Miami CF leads trader consensus for the 2026 MLS Cup at 24.0%, fueled by Lionel Messi's extension through 2025 and the sustained firepower from Luis Suárez, Jordi Alba, and Sergio Busquets, despite their playoff struggles. LAFC sits close at 20.5%, reflecting roster depth with Denis Bouanga and repeated Western Conference finals appearances. The bunched odds highlight MLS parity, playoff unpredictability—as LA Galaxy's 2024 title upset showed—and hype around expansion San Diego FC's (7.7%) marquee signing of Hirving Lozano, plus Columbus Crew's (7.3%) retention of their 2023 championship core amid widespread roster flux and salary cap constraints.

question: Will Russia enter Pokrovskoe by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Pokrovskoe, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, (47.984559° N, 36.228316° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces' incremental advances toward Pokrovsk, a critical Ukrainian logistics hub in Donetsk Oblast, are the primary driver of trader sentiment, with recent captures of villages like Shevchenko and Progress narrowing the gap to under 5 kilometers as of late October 2024. Ukrainian commanders report reinforcing the front amid manpower shortages, while Russian artillery and drone superiority sustain pressure despite seasonal mud hindering mechanized assaults. Western aid deliveries, including ATACMS missiles, bolster defenses but face delays. Traders weigh these dynamics against historical Russian progress rates in the region, with upcoming weather shifts and potential mobilization updates poised to influence the timeline for any encirclement or entry.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Russia entering Khatnie by the specified date shows low implied probability, driven by stalled Russian advances in eastern Ukraine amid Ukrainian counteroffensives and resource strains from the Kursk incursion. Recent developments include intensified fighting near Pokrovsk and Vuhledar, with no reported Russian movements toward western or Kherson-adjacent areas like Khatnie, per official military updates from both sides. Ukrainian forces maintain control in the region following Russia's 2022 withdrawal from the right bank of the Dnipro River. Upcoming catalysts include potential shifts in U.S. military aid, winter weather impacts on operations, and diplomatic talks, all of which traders weigh against historical base rates of slow territorial gains.

question: Will CA Platense win on 2026-03-06?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 6, 2026
If CA Platense wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

events.title: CA Platense vs. Estudiantes de La Plata
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for Friday, March 6, 2026 between CA Platense and Estudiantes de La Plata.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans toward CA Platense at 49.5% implied probability for their home clash against Estudiantes de La Plata, fueled by Platense's unbeaten streak in their last five home matches and a 2-1 victory over Independiente last weekend that boosted momentum. Estudiantes sit at 28%, hampered by a winless run in four away fixtures this season, including a recent 0-0 draw versus Godoy Cruz, exposing defensive frailties on the road. The 32% draw pricing reflects tight head-to-head history—three stalemates in the last five meetings—and both teams' mid-table positioning with little to separate them in Liga Profesional standings. No major injuries reported from official updates, though Platense's rest advantage after midweek could tilt the edge. Upsets remain possible in this evenly matched Buenos Aires derby.

events.title: CA Barracas Central vs. CA Banfield
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for Friday, March 6, 2026 between CA Barracas Central and CA Banfield.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tight odds in the Barracas Central vs. Banfield matchup reflect evenly matched mid-table Argentine Primera División sides, with a draw leading trader consensus at 33% due to both teams' defensive tendencies and recent stalemates. Hosting Barracas Central holds a slight home edge but enters winless in five straight league games, relying on solid backline play that has limited opponents to under 1.5 goals per match lately. Banfield counters with two wins in its last three outings, including a road upset, though poor away form— just one victory in eight—caps upside. Official injury reports show no key absences, head-to-heads favor draws (three of last five), and rest advantages are neutral, sustaining the bunched probabilities.
